commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a final worker-result validation pass in `scripts/run-worker.mjs`, writes a schema-valid blocked result when Codex exits without `result.json`, and fails unrepaired invalid worker results.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: if Codex exits 0 without `result.json`, current main returns from repair, prints a nonexistent result path, and downstream `--latest` consumers throw because no result file exists.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is appropriate because this is a member-authored automation PR with a real workflow handoff risk but no narrow repair finding for ClawSweeper to fix.

target.title: fix: record missing worker results
target.body:
## Summary
- Write a valid blocked result when Codex exits successfully but does not produce result.json.
- Fail the worker job immediately when a produced result remains invalid after repair attempts.
- Include merge_preflight in generated blocked results so they match the worker schema.

## Evidence
- Canary run 27191078727 failed in execute/apply because execute-fix-artifact --latest could not find result.json after the worker step.

## Verification
- node --check scripts/run-worker.mjs
- git diff --check
- npm run test
- npm run validate
- fake codex harness: Codex exits 0 without writing output; run-worker emits a valid blocked result and review-results passes.
target.author: vincentkoc
